The Northman (2022)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $34,233,110Details
International Box Office $35,749,186
Worldwide Box Office $69,982,296
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,089,223
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,829,298
Total $5,918,521
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Young Prince Amleth is on the cusp of becoming a man when his father is brutally murdered by his uncle, who kidnaps the boy’s mother. Fleeing his island kingdom by boat, the child vows revenge. Two decades later, Amleth is a Viking berserker raiding Slavic villages, where a seeress reminds him of his vow: avenge his father, save his mother, kill his uncle. Traveling on a slave ship to Iceland, Amleth infiltrates his uncle’s farm with the help of Olga, an enslaved Slavic woman — and sets out to honor his vow.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$12,290,800 (35.9% of total gross)
Legs:2.79 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:48.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Theater counts:3,234 opening theaters/3,284 max. theaters, 3.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $38,882,056

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 22nd, 2022 (Wide) by Focus Features
International Releases: April 13th, 2022 (Wide) (Sweden)
April 13th, 2022 (Wide) (Norway)
April 13th, 2022 (Wide) (Denmark)
April 14th, 2022 (Wide) (Uruguay)
April 14th, 2022 (Wide) (Slovenia)
Video Release: May 12th, 2022 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong bloody violence, some sexual content and nudity.
(Rating bulletin 2724 (cert #53238), 2/23/2022)
Running Time: 138 minutes
Geographical setting: Iceland
Plot point: Revenge, Vikings
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Regency Enterprises, Focus Features, Perfect World Pictures, New Regency, Square Peg
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Young Prince Amleth is on the cusp of becoming a man when his father is brutally murdered by his uncle, who kidnaps the boy’s mother. Fleeing his island kingdom by boat, the child vows revenge. Two decades later, Amleth is a Viking berserker raiding Slavic villages, where a seeress reminds him of his vow: avenge his father, save his mother, kill his uncle. Traveling on a slave ship to Iceland, Amleth infiltrates his uncle’s farm with the help of Olga, an enslaved Slavic woman — and sets out to honor his vow.
